combat_history.event: INS Sahyadri deployed to evacuate Indian nationals from Yemen during civil unrest, demonstrating long-range deployment capability
combat_history.significance: First operational deployment showing the class's expeditionary capabilities in the Arabian Sea

combat_history.event: Shivalik-class frigates deployed to Ladakh region waters during border tensions, maintaining naval readiness in the Indian Ocean
combat_history.significance: Demonstrated the class's role in India's multi-domain deterrence strategy

combat_history.event: INS Shivalik participated in Quad naval exercises with US, Japanese, and Australian navies, testing interoperability
combat_history.significance: Showcased integration capabilities with allied navies and modern combat systems

modernization.description: Planned upgrades include improved air defense systems, possibly Barak-8 ER replacement for Barak-1, enhanced electronic warfare suite, and combat system updates
modernization.impact: Will extend service life to 2040s and improve air defense coverage significantly

modernization.description: Feasibility study for replacing Klub missiles with indigenous BrahMos supersonic cruise missiles
modernization.impact: Would provide supersonic anti-ship capability throughout flight profile and reduce import dependence

vulnerabilities.description: Barak-1 SAM system provides only 12km range, insufficient against modern anti-ship missiles
vulnerabilities.context: Regional adversaries field missiles with 200+ km range, creating engagement envelope disadvantage
vulnerabilities.mitigation: MLU program considering Barak-8 ER upgrade with 70+ km range

vulnerabilities.description: Relies on single MF-STAR radar for fire control, creating vulnerability if damaged
vulnerabilities.context: Modern naval combat requires redundant sensors for survivability
vulnerabilities.mitigation: No current program to address this limitation due to cost constraints

vulnerabilities.description: Only 24 VLS cells compared to 32-64 on peer frigates, limiting sustained engagement capability
vulnerabilities.context: Modern saturation attacks require higher missile loadout for effective defense
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Follow-on Project 17A increased VLS capacity but no retrofit planned for Shivalik class

operational_patterns.typical_deployment: Independent patrol missions in Indian Ocean, escort duty for replenishment groups, participation in naval exercises
operational_patterns.typical_task_group: Usually operates independently or with replenishment vessel, occasionally as part of carrier battle group
operational_patterns.readiness_notes: High availability rate due to CODOG propulsion reliability, but limited by imported weapons system maintenance requirements

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Type 054A has 32 VLS cells vs 24, but Shivalik has superior stealth design and longer-range anti-ship missiles. Chinese frigate emphasizes air defense while Shivalik balances all mission areas.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Stealth vs firepower - comparing Indo-Pacific frigate design philosophies

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Shivalik significantly outclasses F-22P in stealth, sensors, and weapons range. F-22P is essentially a Chinese export frigate while Shivalik represents indigenous design capability.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: India-Pakistan naval arms race: modern indigenous vs import-dependent capabilities

peer_comparisons.key_differences: FREMM has superior air defense and ASW capabilities, but Shivalik offers better cost-effectiveness and anti-ship strike range. FREMM represents higher-end European design philosophy.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: European vs Indian frigate design: capability vs cost trade-offs

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Both emphasize stealth and multi-mission capability, but Formidable has better air defense (Aster missiles) while Shivalik has longer-range strike capability and larger size for extended operations.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Small navy, big ambitions: comparing Singapore and Indian frigate capabilities
